tax.title: 🇪🇪 Tax System 101 — Estonia (Startup Guide)
intro: Estonia is widely considered one of the most startup-friendly tax systems in the world, mainly because profits are not taxed until they are distributed.
This allows companies to reinvest and scale faster without immediate tax pressure.

sections.heading: 🏢 1. Corporate Income Tax
items: Example: If your startup earns €100,000 and reinvests it → €0 tax. If you distribute €100,000 → €20,000 tax.
Key advantage: You only pay tax when taking money out of the company.

sections.heading: 📈 4. Dividends & Capital Gains
items: No additional tax for individuals if already taxed

sections.heading: 🏠 5. Land Tax (Real Estate)
items: Important: No property tax on buildings. Only land is taxed.

sections.heading: ⛽ 6. Excise Taxes (Fuel, Energy, etc.)
items: Applies if your business involves specific goods:
Fuel (petrol, diesel), electricity & gas, alcohol & tobacco
These are included in prices, not paid separately by most startups.

sections.heading: 🌱 7. Environmental Taxes
items: Carbon-related taxes, mainly via fuel and energy
Packaging taxes, if producing or importing goods

sections.heading: 🏙️ 8. Local / Municipal Taxes
items: Limited in Estonia, but may include:

sections.heading: 🚗 9. Car Tax
items: If your startup provides a company car:
Approx: €1.96 per kW/month × 1.33 social tax multiplier

eresidency.title: E-Residency
eresidency.intro: Information for international founders who want to start and manage an Estonian company online.

sections.heading: What it gives you
items: Digital ID access to Estonian e-services, company administration and online signing.
Useful for founders who want to run an EU company remotely.

sections.heading: Apply
items: Start your application through the official e-Residency portal.
